steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the two authors would disagree about. Find the central tension between Text 1 and Text 2.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The author of Text 2 thinks that among tropical species, females sing less frequently than males do, whereas the author of Text 1 thinks that females sing as frequently as males do.’: Neither text focuses the disagreement on tropical species — this misplaces the comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The author of Text 2 would likely disagree with the author of Text 1 about whether it can be assumed that females of temperate species don't sing.’: Text 1 treats a singing Cassin's finch as evidence of a male, implying females rarely sing in temperate zones. Text 2 states female birdsong is more common among temperate species than currently assumed. Direct conflict. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The author of Text 2 believes that female birdsong is much more common among tropical species than the author of Text 1 believes.’: This reverses which author holds which position. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The author of Text 2 would likely reject the claim by the author of Text 1 that among tropical birds, females sing as frequently as males do.’: Introduces something neither text addresses. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
